title: Conjecture 1.5 (Fox)
statement_text: For every sufficiently large positive integer $n$ and every $n$-vertex perfect graph $G$, there is a pure pair $A, B$ in $G$ with $|A|, |B| \geq n^{1-o(1)}$.
context_text: Fox proved (result 1.3) that every $n$-vertex comparability graph has a pure pair $A, B$ with $|A|, |B| > \frac{n}{4\log_2 n}$, and also that there exist comparability graphs with no pure pair of size $\geq \frac{15n}{\log_2 n}$. Since comparability graphs are perfect, Fox conjectured the same sub-polynomial lower bound should extend to all perfect graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves this conjecture in full; specifically establishes (1.6) that for all $c > 0$ and sufficiently large $n$, every $n$-vertex perfect graph $G$ admits a pure pair $A, B$ with $|A|, |B| \geq n^{1-c}$, and further strengthens it (1.7–1.9) by making one side linear, relaxing the perfect graph hypothesis to excluding a single long hole and a single long antihole, and ultimately excluding any graph of sufficiently large branch-length and its complement.
paper_title: Pure pairs. V. Excluding some long subdivision
